purpose: Classify current ProtoKits into a gated DSK/domain incubation pipeline.

entries.currentAction: Keep as the renderer-agnostic boundary between host tracking observations and committed spatial anchors.
entries.nextLedge: Adopt the proven WebXR adapter in two executable hosts and capture device-level wall/floor proof before promotion review.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not absorb raw XR frames, camera streams, marker libraries, renderer reticles, anchors, objectives, or game copy.

entries.currentAction: Keep as the explicit structural WebXR frame/result/pose adapter feeding spatial-surface-candidate.
entries.nextLedge: Integrate in Lost Pages and one independent WebXR host, then capture real-device hit-test and replay evidence.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not absorb XR session lifecycle, candidate policy, anchors, renderer reticles, objectives, or game copy.

entries.currentAction: Use as the canonical serializable committed-anchor descriptor owner under engine.n.anchorDescriptors.
entries.nextLedge: Exercise snapshot migration and committed placement consumption in two executable hosts before promotion review.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not absorb host XRAnchor lifecycle, persistence transport, surface discovery, rendering, or game-specific anchor meaning.

entries.currentAction: Use as the canonical serializable repository, kit, domain, and bundle manifest owner under engine.n.kitRegistry.
entries.nextLedge: Promote with capability graph and composition planning, then add repository transport and code-loading only in stable installer adapters.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not absorb fetch, GitHub resolution, caching transport, module import, code execution, installation, filesystem writes, rendering, or game behavior.
entries.purgeDecision: kit-registry, kit-manifest-domain-kit, and domain-manifest-registry-domain-kit retain compatibility exports but delegate to this canonical state owner.

entries.currentAction: Preserve the complete old utility import surface through a native stateless compatibility DSK while canonical ownership stays in NexusEngine.
entries.nextLedge: Promote only as a deprecated compatibility entry with exact replacement lineage, then migrate source kits toward native NexusEngine owners.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not make protokit-core an official catch-all foundation domain or add new behavior to it.
entries.purgeDecision: Keep utility compatibility behavior, replace runtime/math/random/serialization ownership claims with NexusEngine contracts, and prevent default installation.

entries.currentAction: Use as the canonical world-seed and bounded named deterministic stream owner under engine.n.seedStream.
entries.nextLedge: Promote the validated service as seed-kit while preserving the generic factory and API aliases for compatibility.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not absorb procedural generation, loot policy, encounter policy, content, rendering, persistence transport, networking, or entropy.
entries.purgeDecision: Replace the generic catch-all runtime with a native demand-driven DSK composed over NexusEngine seeded-random primitives.

entries.currentAction: Use as the sole requires/provides/composes graph, missing-dependency, cycle, cluster, and deterministic install-order owner.
entries.nextLedge: Promote beside the canonical registry and keep batch registration plus 1,000-node proof green.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not fetch registries, import modules, install kits, mutate files, or own child capability behavior.

entries.currentAction: Use as the deterministic kit/domain/bundle selection and install-plan owner over registry and capability graph contracts.
entries.nextLedge: Promote as the planning boundary consumed by the stable installer without moving installation side effects into the DSK.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not fetch, import, execute, install, write files, render, or own child domain rules.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a renderer-agnostic pressure-channel DSK and verify replay plus multi-config usage before Core review.
entries.nextLedge: Keep smoke and replay coverage green and identify the next downstream consumer.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not merge route fiction or renderer feedback into this atomic boundary.

entries.currentAction: Use as the canonical NexusEngine runtime resource-meter collection under engine.n.resourceMeter, with genericResourceLoop aliases retained for compatibility.
entries.nextLedge: Promote the proven 1.0.0 behavior into NexusEngine-Kits, then migrate pressure and resource-pressure compatibility layers to compose it without deleting their distinct policy behavior.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not absorb pressure policy, economy, inventory, cargo fiction, host input, rendering, or persistence transport.
entries.purgeDecision: Replace the stable catalog placeholder only; retain pressure owners and the old resource-pressure compatibility surface until separate parity-backed migrations exist.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a reusable timing window and cooldown DSK.
entries.nextLedge: Preserve deterministic fixtures for accepted and rejected timing windows.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not add game-specific tutorial timing or renderer feedback.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a renderer-agnostic target availability and prompt descriptor DSK.
entries.nextLedge: Use as a scan, survey, or interaction child boundary where Experiments need affordance descriptors.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not let prompt descriptors become UI rendering or game-specific tutorial copy.

entries.currentAction: Keep as the smallest reusable route/checkpoint/objective-progress boundary.
entries.nextLedge: Keep next-ledge partial consumption stable and add route-level replay before any Core promotion claim.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not build another route/checkpoint atom or fold cargo/resource/pressure into this kit.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a composite coordinator over route progress, resource loop, and pressure loop child DSKs.
entries.nextLedge: Consume one cargo/resource/pressure source from next-ledge through this composite before claiming a full traversal/cargo-pressure lane.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not promote the composite or claim a second executable lane before downstream route proof.

entries.currentAction: Use as the preferred atomic alias surface for generic-defense route-host migration.
entries.nextLedge: Keep Signal Bastion consuming the namespaced child boundaries while preserving compatibility aliases.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not promote the broad generic-defense bundle as a single Core kit.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a migration bridge for hosts that need broad compatibility plus atomic DSK aliases.
entries.nextLedge: Use it to keep hosts working while route calls migrate toward engine.n.genericDefense child boundaries.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not treat the AAA bridge as Core-promotion-ready.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a narrow command extension for setBlueprint and sell/refund while compatibility risk remains.
entries.nextLedge: Keep Signal Bastion proving package import and n.genericDefense session command calls.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not fold into Core or sessionFacade until compatibility risk is low and replay stays green.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a game-family incubation stack whose atomic children prove climb, swing, risk, input, camera, and feedback boundaries separately.
entries.nextLedge: Preserve Next Ledge as preset/composition while identifying which child boundary has the cleanest promotion path.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not promote next-ledge-kit as a branded whole-game kit.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a generic racing family stack and review child domains separately.
entries.nextLedge: Classify slope traversal, boost path, racer contact, and race hazard as separate promotion-facing child candidates only after proof.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not promote the full arcade race family as one Core kit.

entries.currentAction: Split environment descriptors, world patching, flight traversal, and renderer-facing batch descriptors into independently reviewed domains.
entries.nextLedge: Separate simulation/state domains from descriptor-only domains in future promotion reviews.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not promote the whole open-world flight stack as one Core capability.

entries.currentAction: Keep as a renderer-agnostic stereo descriptor domain for host adapters.
entries.nextLedge: Keep descriptor-only tests green and avoid adding raw platform session ownership.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not turn descriptor state into a renderer adapter or platform runtime.

entries.currentAction: Keep as visual descriptor and layer-state output for hosts, not a drawing engine.
entries.nextLedge: Keep visual pipeline descriptor-only while consumers prove host renderer separation.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not move renderer implementation or gameplay outcomes into visual descriptor kits.

entries.currentAction: Hold as a maximum-feature spatial-platformer-loop incubation suite and review child domains separately.
entries.nextLedge: Add deterministic replay for one child platformer boundary or a suite-level fixed-tick fixture before any promotion-facing claim.
entries.doNotDoNext: Do not promote the full suite or use suite smoke coverage as a Core-promotion signal.
